title: UI/UX Decision Guide — Research-Backed Implementation
description: Implements 6 research-backed UI/UX improvements to the Weekly Commit Module: semantic colors, progressive disclosure, AI suggestion tiering, dual font stack, charting library migration, and loading/empty states. Based on analysis of enterprise patterns (Linear, Notion, GitHub) and WCAG 2.1 AA guidelines.

blockingIssues.title: Decision 1 (Semantic Colors) underestimates scope — plan says 'swap 6 CSS vars' but actual change is far larger
blockingIssues.detail: The plan claims 'highest impact, lowest effort — swap 6 CSS vars'. In reality: (1) index.css has 3 light-mode color vars (success/warning/danger) but needs 12 new tokens (base/bg/border × 4 categories), (2) dark mode needs 12 additional overrides in the [data-theme='dark'] block, (3) the Badge component uses foreground/alpha patterns (e.g., 'bg-foreground/5 text-foreground') not color tokens — all 9 Badge variants need rewriting, (4) ~30+ places in 15+ files use text-success/text-warning/text-danger/bg-danger etc. that need updating, (5) Button variants (danger, success) reference these tokens, (6) Toast component uses semantic color classes. This is a medium-effort change spanning the entire component library, not a '6 CSS vars' swap.

blockingIssues.title: No Collapsible/Accordion component exists — Decision 2 requires building one
blockingIssues.detail: The plan assumes wrapping sections in 'collapsible containers' but the ui/ component library has no Collapsible or Accordion component. The only collapsible pattern in the codebase is ad-hoc useState-based toggling (EvidenceDrawer, WhatIfPanel, Plan History). A reusable CollapsibleSection component needs to be created first, supporting: default open/closed state, localStorage persistence of expansion state, summary/badge slot for collapsed preview, 'Expand all / Collapse all' coordination. This is a prerequisite step the plan doesn't mention.

blockingIssues.title: Decision 4 (Typography) requires adding Inter font — no Inter/Geist Sans exists in the project
blockingIssues.detail: The plan recommends Inter as the primary proportional font, but the project only loads Geist Mono via Google Fonts CDN. Neither Inter nor Geist Sans is referenced anywhere. Steps needed: (1) add Inter font import to index.css or install it as a package, (2) change --font-family-base, (3) Tailwind v4's @theme needs the new value, (4) body already uses var(--font-family-base), so the base swap propagates, BUT (5) many components (commit titles, ticket IDs, etc.) need explicit font-mono class added since they currently inherit the mono font from body. The plan doesn't list which components need explicit font-mono annotation.

blockingIssues.title: Decision 5 (Charts) doesn't account for Tremor v3 → v4 API changes and Tailwind v4 compatibility
blockingIssues.detail: The project uses Tailwind CSS v4 (@tailwindcss/vite 4.2.2) which uses the new CSS-first configuration (@theme instead of tailwind.config.js). Tremor (recommended library) historically requires tailwind.config.js and has specific plugin setup. Need to verify Tremor v3/v4 is compatible with Tailwind v4's new config system. If not, Recharts (framework-agnostic) would be the safer choice. The plan should specify the exact version and verify compatibility.

blockingIssues.title: Decision 3 (AI Suggestion UX) — 'ghost/preview state' for ReconcileAssist is architecturally complex
blockingIssues.detail: The plan recommends changing Reconcile pre-fill from 'auto-fills outcomes' to 'ghost/preview state — show suggested outcomes as faded text that solidifies on click'. However, examining ReconcileAssistPanel.tsx and Reconcile.tsx: the current pattern is explicit opt-in (user clicks 'AI Assist Reconciliation' button, then clicks per-row 'Apply' buttons). The isAiSuggested prop already exists on ReconcileCommitRow with AiSuggestedBadge visual distinction. The plan's recommendation is largely already implemented — this should be removed or clarified.

blockingIssues.title: Decision 2 — localStorage persistence needs a shared hook/utility
blockingIssues.detail: The plan says 'persist expansion state to localStorage' but doesn't address the implementation pattern. Existing localStorage usage (sidebar collapse, theme) uses direct calls. For 10+ collapsible sections each with persistence, a reusable useLocalStorageState hook should be created. Without this, each section will have redundant boilerplate.

blockingIssues.title: Decision 3 — 'per-component dismiss memory' and 'global AI preference' need backend/context support
blockingIssues.detail: The plan recommends: (1) per-component dismiss memory (if user dismisses lint 3x, collapse by default), (2) global AI preference toggle to reduce suggestions to 'on-demand only'. Neither of these has any existing infrastructure. The dismiss counter needs localStorage or server persistence. The global AI preference needs a new settings page or admin panel addition (currently Admin.tsx handles org config, not user preferences). The featureFlags.aiAssistanceEnabled flag is org-level, not per-user. These are non-trivial new features that need their own implementation steps.

blockingIssues.title: Decision 6 (Empty States) mentions 'illustration' but no illustration system exists
blockingIssues.detail: The plan says empty states should include 'illustration + description + CTA'. The project uses lucide-react for icons but has no illustration system, no SVG illustrations, and no illustration component. Either: (a) scope this to icon + description + CTA (more realistic), or (b) add an illustration step. The plan should clarify this.

blockingIssues.title: Testing impact not addressed — existing tests will break
blockingIssues.detail: The Badge.test.tsx tests check for specific className content (e.g., 'bg-muted', 'text-foreground', 'text-muted'). Changing Badge variants for semantic colors will break these tests. MyWeekPage.test.tsx has extensive mocking and DOM assertions. The plan needs explicit test-update steps for each decision, especially Decision 1 (Badge tests, Button tests, Toast tests) and Decision 2 (MyWeek page test restructuring).

nonBlockingNotes: Decision 1's proposed color values should be contrast-checked against WCAG AA. The plan mentions WCAG but doesn't verify the proposed muted colors meet 4.5:1 contrast ratios (e.g., #5F7A66 on #EEF3EF = ~3.3:1 which fails AA for normal text).

The existing dark mode already uses vibrant semantic colors (#4ade80 success, #fbbf24 warning, #f87171 danger) unlike the muted palette proposed. The plan should decide: harmonize light+dark to both be muted, or accept the existing dark mode colors.
Decision 2's WhatIfPanel is described as 'already collapsed (good ✓)' which matches — it uses useState(false) for isExpanded.
Decision 5 could leverage the existing pure CSS chart infrastructure for a phased migration rather than a wholesale rewrite.

The project is a micro-frontend (Module Federation) — any new dependencies (Inter font, Tremor/Recharts) need to be checked against the shared dependency list to avoid duplication in the host app bundle.
Decision 6's three-tier loading strategy (skeleton < 2s, progress 2-5s, explicit > 5s) needs a timeout wrapper utility — this doesn't exist yet.
